“TREE HOUSE OF HORROR III" Written by Jay Kogen & Wallace Wolodarsky Al Jean & Mike Reiss

Sam Simon Jon Vitti

FADE IN:

INT. THEATRE - (THE SAME OPENING SHOT FROM THE LAST TWO HALLOWEEN SHOWS)

HOMER steps out from behind the curtain. HOMER

Hello. I’ve been asked to tell you that the following show is very scary, with stuff that might give your kids nightmares. "So what’s the problem?" you may ask. Well, there are some cry-babies out there -- religious types mostly -- who might be offended. If you are one of then, I advise you to turn off your set now.

A scary looking ZOMBIE with an ax embedded in his head staggers out from behind the curtains.

ZOMBIE

(LOW, SCARY VOICE) Am I on yet? HOMER

(MOCKING ZOMBIE VOICE) No, you‘re not

on yet.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

ZOMBIE (GROWLS MENACINGLY)

As the Zombie walks off, he has trouble finding the opening in the curtain.

HOMER (TO SELF) Stupid Zombie. (TO CAMERA) Enjoy the show.

Homer tries to find the opening to the curtain, but can’t. He and the zombie flail around, GROWLING.

HOMER (CONT’D) (TO ZOMBIE) What’d they do? Try

over there.

ZOMBIE Where?

HOMER Over there.

ZOMBIE

(GROWLS ) SUPER: "THE SIMPSONS HALLOWEEN SPECIAL III" EXT. CEMETERY - NIGHT [SAME ESTABLISHING SHOT AS LAST YEAR]

PAN through the tombstones which read: "DREXELL’S CLASS", "AMERICAN WORKMANSHIP" (The tombstone collapses into rubble), "RETIRED AND LOVING IT" and "ERNEST BURNS IN HELL".

The camera moves through the cemetery to the Simpsons’ house. It PUSHES IN to the:

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM

Like the end of our opening credits, FIVE SIMPSON-SHAPED SKELETONS run in and sit in front of the TV. The baby skeleton SUCKS a pacifier. [NOTE: FIRST COMMERCIAL BREAK COMES AFTER CREDITS. ]

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE = LIVING ROOM - HALLOWEEN NIGHT A kids’ Halloween party is in progress. Homer comes down the stairs, dressed in a toga with a laurel wreath around his head. HOMER Behold mighty Caesar, in all his glory!

Homer’s toga gets caught on a nail, RIPPING it off. He is now only wearing his underwear. All the KIDS LAUGH.

HOMER (CONT’D) (ANNOYED GRUNT) ANGLE ON BART BART is dressed as a Droog from "Clockwork Orange." BART Real ‘orror show!

LISA walks up, dressed as the Statue of Liberty holding the torch in one arm and books in the other.

LISA I lift my lamp beside the golden door. Bart waves his hand in front of his nose. BART Hey Lis, put down your arm, I’m yearning to breathe free. Lisa BONKS him with her torch. BART

Oww.

3.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

ANGLE ON - ANOTHER AREA MARTIN, dressed in a frilly costume with wings and flowers, approaches NELSON, who wears a pirate’s eye patch and bandana. MARTIN I am Calliope, the muse of heroic poetry. NELSON No kiddin’. (HITTING NOISE) Nelson PUNCHES Martin, who falls to the ground, MOANING. DISSOLVE TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - A LITTLE LATER

The kids are sitting in chairs in a line. Marge, dressed like Cleopatra, is leading them in a game.

MARGE You children may not know it, but there was once an evil witch, and she died a horrible death.

MARTIN How?

MARGE (WINGING IT) Well... she had this thing on her hand. Didn’t think it was much. By the time she went to her doctor, it was too late. (MURMURS )

Lisa shuts off the light. Marge CLICKS on a flashlight and uses it to see a plate of food on her lap.

4.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

MARGE (CONT’D) These are her eyes...

She plucks a couple of grapes off a bunch and hands them to the nearest CHILD. The kids make GROSSED OUT NOISES.

MARGE (CONT’D) And this is her hair... Marge passes some spaghetti down the line. MARGE (CONT’D) (SCARY) And these are her brains!

Marge passes a raw piece of meat down the line. After a BEAT:

BART (V.0.) Yo Mom, we haven’t got the eye balls yet. Marge CLICKS on the lights. Homer is in the middle of the line with spaghetti hanging out of his mouth, about to bite the meat. MARGE Homer, you’re ruining it. HOMER (COVERING) Yeah... well... it was an evil game. MARGE Well, now we need another Halloween activity. BART In Detroit, it’s customary to burn

down your own neighborhood.

5.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

MARGE I don’t think so, Bart. Um... Does anyone know a ghost story?

LISA Ido. It’s the story of a boy and

his doll. A doll... From Hell! (EVIL

LAUGH) DISSOLVE TO: ACT ONE: "DOLL OF DEATH"

MUSIC: OMINOUS CLOSE UP - A BOX

Two hands slowly slide the lid off. They reach in to pull out a hideous monster head.

MUSIC: DRAMATIC STING PULL BACK to reveal: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY A banner on the wall reads, "Happy Birthday Bart!" Bart is unwrapping presents. He holds up the monster head which is a rubber novelty mask. Bart puts the mask on and turns to Maggie. BART

(MONSTER NOISES) ow! Maggie sticks her finger through the mask’s eye hole, poking Bart in the eye. Bart takes the mask off and looks at his pile of presents.

BART (CONT'D)

Hey Homer - where’s your present?

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

HOMER (ANNOYED GRUNT) I mean don’t worry, son. I forgot to get you a present this year. But I swear on my father’s grave... GRAMPA Hey! HOMER I will get you one now! Homer strides out the door. EXT. STORE - EVENING The sign reads "HOUSE OF EVIL -- YOUR ONE-STOP EVIL SHOP." Homer pulls up, glances at the sign, makes an INTERESTED NOISE and walks in. INT. HOUSE OF EVIL - NIGHT The store is filled with strange bric-a-brac. Homer approaches the SHOPKEEPER, an old Keye Luke type in an ornately embroidered silk robe. HOMER Do you sell toys? SHOPKEEPER (OMINOUS) We sell forbidden objects from places men fear to tread. (CHEERFUL) We also sell frozen yogurt... (INDICATES YOGURT MACHINE) which I call frogurt! HOMER Well, I need something for my son’s

birthday.

7.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

SHOPKEEPER Perhaps this will please the gentleman. The Shopkeeper reaches up on a shelf holding: a shrunken head labeled "20% rayon, 80% shrunken head"; a stuffed, two-headed cat; a beating heart in a jar; and a 16-inch Krusty the Clown doll. He brings down the doll. HOMER (EXAMINING DOLL) I don’t know. It’s kinda creepy lookin’. Lemme see the beating heart in the jar. The shopkeeper hands the jar to Homer. SHOPKEEPER This heart will provide the owner with eternal life. All for only $9.95! HOMER Ten bucks for a heart? Go to hell. SHOPKEEPER (OFFENDED) Take the doll with ny compliments. May it bring you years of misery and pain. HOMER Wait a minute. What’s the catch? SHOPKEEPER It’s cursed. HOMER

But it is free?

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

SHOPKEEPER (EXASPERATED) Yes. HOMER (TAKING DOLL) Heh, heh, so long, sucker. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT There is a party going on. Bart’s friends look on as a blindfolded little boy is trying to pin the tail ona picture of a donkey which is taped to the front door. Just as the boy reaches the picture, Homer BURSTS in, knocking the boy over. HOMER (HANDING BART THE GIFT) Happy birthday, son!

Bart unwraps the gift, and pulls out the doll. The crowd "Oohs.""

BART Great Caesar’s Ghost! A talking Krusty doll!

Bart pulls the doll’s string.

KRUSTY DOLL I’m Krusty the Clown and I love you very much.

BART Oh, Dad, this is the best birthday I‘ve ever had.

Homer and Bart hug. The tender tableau is broken by Grampa, hunched over a cane.

9.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

GRAMPA That doll is evil, I tells you. Evil! EEEVIL! MARGE Grampa, you said that about all the presents. GRAMPA (SADLY) I just want attention. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY

Homer is watching stock car racing on television.

HOMER Come on... come on... come on... one more... SFX: CRASH HOMER (CONT’D) All right!

ANNOUNCER (V.0.) (GRIM) Ladies and gentlemen, a grisly record has just been set: this is the biggest crash in the history of the Daytona 500! HOMER Woo hoo! Homer hears a NOISE. He turns to see the doll is suddenly sitting next to him. Homer starts and then CHUCKLES at

himself. He picks up the doll and pulls the string in its back.

10.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

KRUSTY DOLL I’m Krusty the Clown and I don’t like you. HOMER Heh, heh, heh. KRUSTY DOLL I’m Krusty the Clown and I’m going to kill you! HOMER (CHUCKLES) Didn’t even pull the string that time. KRUSTY DOLL I said I’m going to kill you. You, Homer Simpson. I’m gonna slice your fat belly wide open. HOMER (OFFENDED) Hey. The doll leaps at Homer wielding a large knife. HOMER (CONT’D) (SCREAMS ) The family runs in. MARGE Homer, what’s wrong? HOMER (NEAR TEARS) That doll tried to kill me!

The doll is laying limply on the couch.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BART I’d say that the pressure has finally gotten to Dad, but what pressure? HOMER No, it really did try to kill me. Listen! Homer pulls the string. KRUSTY DOLL I’m Krusty the Clown and I love you very much. MARGE/LISA/ BART Awww. Marge, Lisa and Bart go into the kitchen. HOMER Don’t leave me alone with him! KRUSTY DOLL See ya in hell, Tubbs. (MANIACAL LAUGH) The doll LAUGHS INSANELY then stops abruptly. He points to the string behind his back. Homer pulls it. The doll resumes its MANIACAL LAUGH. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - BATHROOM Homer sits in the tub. HOMER (SINGING TO THE TUNE OF "FLIPPER") They call me Homer, Homer, King of the sea,

For no one, you see,

12.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

Is smarter than me... The Krusty Doll springs out of the water at him. HOMER (CONT’D) (SCREAMS) Homer jumps out of the tub and runs out of the bathroon. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE = LIVING ROOM = DAY

Homer streaks, naked, wet and dripping past Patty, Selma and Marge.

PATTY (GROSSED OUT) We won’t be staying for dinner. INT. NUCLEAR POWER PLANT - DAY Homer looks at his watch. CLOSE UP - THE WATCH It reads 9:30. HOMER (V.O.) Well, it’s almost lunch time, heh, heh, heh. BACK TO SCENE Homer opens his lunch box and the Krusty Doll leaps out at him. The doll repeatedly BANGS Homer’s head against the console. HOMER (HORRIFIED) What have you done with my lunch? INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LISA’S ROOM - DAY

The Krusty doll is talking to Lisa’s Malibu Stacie doll, in her doll house. He has his arm around her.

13.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

KRUSTY DOLL Hey, baby. Get comfortable. Relax. It’s a little hot for that cheerleader outfit, don’t you think?

Homer BURSTS in the room with a laundry bag. He tosses the Krusty doll into the laundry bag and ZIPS it closed.

KRUSTY DOLL (V.0.) You think your dirty socks can stop me? Well... they are makin’ me dizzy... (WOOZY NOISE)

Homer shoves the laundry bag into a suitcase and chains the suitcase closed.

EXT. FIELD - DAY

We see a sign labelled, "Caution: Bottomless Pit." Homer drops the suitcase in the bottomless pit.

HOMER Goodbye, Dolly.

As he exits, a GANGSTER carries a man-sized bag to the pit. eee struggles. The gangster drops the bag into the GANGSTER

Arrivederci, Vito. A MAN drops his garbage into the pit. MAN These old newspapers and tin cans won’t be bothering me any more. EXT. ROAD - DAY

Homer is driving down the road, nervously checking the rear view mirror.

14.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

INT. SIMPSON GARAGE - DAY Homer pulls into the garage. HOMER

And that is that. Homer dusts his palms and walks into the house. PAN DOWN the car to see the doll has strapped himself to the bottom of the car a la Robert DeNiro in "Cape Fear." The doll unbuckles himself from the drive train of the car. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY

Homer enters. The doll is waiting on top of the doorway. He jumps on Homer’s head and covers his eyes.

KRUSTY DOLL Guess who, Fat Boy?

They begin to struggle. Marge enter the room and sees Homer rolling around with the doll.

MARGE Oh my God.

The doll is forcing Homer’s head into the dog’s water dish, trying to drown him. Homer pulls his head out.

HOMER (SPUTTERING) Ew. Dog water. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY

A worried Marge is dialing the phone. She holds the doll’s box which reads, "Problems? Call: 1-909-DONT SUE."

MARGE (ON THE PHONE) Your doll is trying to kill my husband!... Yes, I/11 hold. We hear a song while Marge is on hold. SINGER (V.0O.) Everybody loves a clown / So why

don’t you?

15.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM = DAY Marge opens the door for a REPAIRMAN. The doll is bracing its legs on either side of Homer’s mouth and is trying to yank out Homer’s tongue. REPAIRMAN (WISE GUY VOICE) Yep. Here’s your problem.

The repairman takes the doll and lifts up its shirt revealing a switch.

REPAIRMAN (CONT’D) Someone set this thing to "EVIL". He flicks the switch to "GOOD." KRUSTY DOLL I love you, Homer. HOMER (WARMLY) Come here you. Homer hugs the doll. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY Homer watches TV. The Krusty Doll comes in HUFFING and PUFFING, carrying a big tray of food over his head that he sets in front of Homer. KRUSTY DOLL Here ya’ go buddy. HOMER (MOANS) This is light beer. Do you mind makin’ another trip? KRUSTY DOLL

(CLENCHED TEETH) No, not at all.

16.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

INT. MALIBU STACEY DOLL HOUSE - A LITTLE LATER

The Krusty Doll comes home to Malibu Stacey. He hangs up his coat.

KRUSTY DOLL Oh, what a day. Homer made me give him a sponge bath. (SHUDDERS) But coming home to you makes it all worth while. He gives her a kiss. Her head falls off. KRUSTY DOLL (CONT’D) Oh, oh. Here, let me get that for you. He puts her head back on and gives her a hug. HEART-SHAPED IRIS OUT: FADE OUT.

END OF ACT ONE

LT.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

ACT TWO: "KING HOMER"

FADE IN: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM A scarily lit Homer is wrapping up a story. HOMER ... So then, his wife comes through the door!!! BART So? HOMER Did I mention she was dead? LISA No. HOMER Well, she was. (SCARY) And she hit him in the head with a golf club!

BART

HOMER Don’t you remember? He went golfing all the time and it really bugged her.

LISA You said he went bowling!

HOMER (ANNOYED GRUNT)

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

GRAMPA Homer, I’ve coughed up scarier stuff than that.

BART Grampa, why don’t you tell us a story? You’ve led an interesting life.

GRAMPA That’s a lie and you know it. But I have seen a lot of movies...

DISSOLVE TO: EXT. SPRINGFIELD HARBOR ~ ESTABLISHING

(NOTE: THE FOLLOWING IS DONE IN BLACK AND WHITE ‘30’S MOVIE STYLE, RIPPING OFF "KING KONG" SHAMELESSLY]

EXT. DOCK - CONTINUOUS MR. BURNS, in natty white suit and pith helmet, stands by a ship, with SMITHERS. SAILORS load items onto the ship including crates labelled "RIFLES," "GAS BOMBS" and "BILGE RATS." MARGE, in ’30’s garb, approaches holding a newspaper. MARGE My name is Marge Bouvier. I’m here about your ad. CLOSE UP - AD Marge reads the following. MARGE (V.0.) "Single white female wanted for

mysterious expedition. Must like

monkeys. Non-smoker preferred."

19.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BACK TO SCENE BURNS Well, you’d be a welcome change of pace from the rest of these crude and uncouth sailors.

We see two SAILORS are having an argument. One pokes the other in the chest.

SAILOR #1 Cubism is nothin’ but a cheap gimmick.

SAILOR #2 Me father was a cubist!

He SMASHES a bottle and lunges at Sailor #1. They wrestle, making GRUNTING noises.

BURNS (RE: MARGE) What do you think Smithers? SMITHERS (SOUR) I think women and seamen don’t mix. BURNS We know what you think. Young lady, you're hired. (SINISTER LAUGH) MARGE (NERVOUS) What’s this expedition

about?

20.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BURNS It’s about adventure! Danger lurking around every corner! MARGE But what are we going after? BURNS (LYING) Uh... sponges. EXT. SHIP - DAY The boat is in the middle of the ocean. Marge is ina "30's style bathing suit, sunning herself in a deck chair. PAN UP to crewmen LENNY, CARL and CHARLIE, looking on from an upper deck. CARL (CHEERFUL) Hey, I heard we’re going to Zombie Island. LENNY Ah, there’s no such place. We’re going to Monster Island. CHARLIE You’re crazy. We’re going to Skull Island. CARL Ooh, I don’t like the sound of that. MUSIC: DRAMATIC STING EXT. SHIP - DAY The ship docks at an island with a looming mountain shaped

exactly like a skull. The ship’s Captain, OTTO, turns to Burns.

21s.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

OTTO Hey, man, why do they call this Skull Island? BURNS It was discovered in 1792 by Admiral Artemis skull. We hear DRUMBEATS and NATIVES CHANTING "Homer, Homer..." BURNS (CONT’D) Hear that? We/’re going ashore. Marge comes up in her bathing suit. MARGE Am I going too? BURNS Of course. We wouldn’t think of going without the bait... uh, that is, the bait-thing beauty -- The bathing beauty. (PLEASED) I covered that up pretty well. EXT. SKULL ISLAND -- LATER

The members of the expedition crouch behind a bush. SOUTH SEA NATIVES are doing a ritual dance.

NATIVES Homer... Homer... Homer...

A CHIEF presides over the ceremony. The natives stop when they see Marge’s hair poking way out over the bush.

CHIEF

Mose tatupu! Mose tatupu!

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

SUBTITLE: THE WOMAN WITH THE BLUE HAIR WILL MAKE A GOOD SACRIFICE.

MARGE What’s he saying? BURNS (COVERING) He’s saying, "Welcome to Skull Island -- The happiest place on earth!" MARGE (HAPPY) Oh, well, isn’t that... Whoa! She is whisked off by TWO NATIVES. EXT. ISLAND - A LITTLE LATER

Marge is tied to two posts atop a giant wall as a sacrifice.

BACK TO SCENE

The ceremony is in full swing. Atop the wall, a native BANGS a giant gong.

INT. JUNGLE - CONTINUOUS

A fat bald KING KONG-LIKE HOMER (40 feet tall) wrestles a DINOSAUR.

HOMER (MUTTERED NOISES AND CURSES)

Homer is distracted by the GONG. The dinosaur bites his hand.

HOMER (CONT’D) (ANNOYED GRUNT) Natives HUSH as they hear "DOH!" echo through the jungle.

Homer tosses the dinosaur aside and heads for the wall. When Marge sees him, she SCREAMS.

23.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

EXT. GIANT WALL - CONTINUOUS Burns and Smithers look on in awe. BURNS

Smithers, this is a golden opportunity. If we get him alive we can put him on Broadway. Dead, we can make hats!

ON HOMER

He has the SCREAMING Marge in his hand. He bends her hair down with his finger and it POPS up again.

HOMER (CHUCKLES) Ooo. MARGE You keep your hands to yourself, mister. Homer holds her up to his nose and SNIFFS her hair. HOMER (BOB HOPE PURR) Homer smiles a big toothy grin. MARGE (GIGGLES DEMURELY) Suddenly, the gates open, and the expedition members rush in with weapons. They FIRE ON Homer. Homer GROWLS ANGRILY, then turns back to Marge. A cannonball hits Homer in the head. He ROARS and sets Marge in a tree. He picks up Lenny, who fired the cannon, and starts to CHEW HIM UP. LENNY Hey Homer, cut it out. Come on, quit

eating me.

24.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

Carl FIRES a rifle at Homer. It hits Lenny in Homer’s mouth.

LENNY (CONT’D) Ow! (SARCASTIC) Nice shot, Carl. Homer SWALLOWS Lenny and BURPS. ANGLE ON BURNS He wields a gas bomb. BURNS All right, you big ape, get a snootful of this gas bomb. Burns throws it about two feet. Gas pours out, dazing him. BURNS (CONT’D) (MOANS ) BACK TO SCENE Carl is CRUSHED under Homer’s giant foot. CARL (BACK-CRUSHING GROAN) ANGLE ON BURNS Lying on his back, loopy from the gas. BURNS (SINGING) You can bring cake and porterhouse steaks / But don’t bring Lulu / Oh, no. / Lulu gets blue and she goes cuckoo -- Smithers throws another gas bomb. ON HOMER

The gas bomb EXPLODES. Homer SCREAMS, then collapses.

25.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

HOMER (CONTENTED SLEEPING NOISE) Burns and Smithers look at him. BURNS Nice work, Smithers. When we get back, I’m giving you a raise. Homer grabs Smithers, EATS him, and DOZES OFF again. BURNS (CONT’D) Oh well. SMASH CUT TO: EXT. A SPRINGFIELD THEATER An opening night black tie crowd mills in front. The marquee reads "KING HOMER -- EIGHTH WONDER OF THE WORLD" "PLUS: THE CHUBBIEST KICK LINE IN TOWN." INT. THEATER - BACK STAGE = CONTINUOUS CHUBBY DANCERS are kicking their way offstage. Mr. Burns is in top hat and tails. Marge wears a gown and mink stole. She looks at a GROWLING, chained up Homer, o.s. Thirties’ style REPORTERS and PHOTOGRAPHERS approach. 430'S REPORTER

What kinda show you got for us, Mr.

Burns?

26.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BURNS Well, the ape’s going to stand around for about an hour or so, then a fifteen minute intermission. Then more ape-standing -- another intermission -- and then for the grand finale, the organist plays a Gershwin Medley while the ape listens appreciatively. *30'S REPORTER Sensational! INT. THEATER - ON STAGE - A LITTLE LATER Burns stands on stage before a giant curtain. BURNS Ladies and gentlemen, in his native land he was a king. But he comes before you in chains, for your own amusement. Presenting Homer, the Eighth Wonder of the World!

The curtain goes up, revealing Homer, on a platforn, chained to a post. The audience GASPS.

IN AUDIENCE BARNEY Wow! Look at the size of that platforn! ON STAGE

The reporters are taking FLASH photographs. Homer ROARS and strains at his chains.

27.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

MARGE I think you’re making him angry.

DAVE SHUTTON Come on, what’s he gonna do, run amok in downtown Springfield?

Homer breaks free of his chains. He STEPS ON Dave Shutton. Pandemonium ensues. Barney holds up a bag of peanuts.

BARNEY Hey monkey, want a peanut? Homer grabs the bag. BARNEY (CONT’D) I said one!

He SMACKS Homer in the shin repeatedly. Homer walks off, CRASHING through a wall.

INT. ANOTHER THEATER - CONTINUOUS A CROWD watches a ’30’s little GIRL MOVIE STAR on stage. GIRL STAR

(SINGS) On the good ship,

Lollipop / It’s a sweet trip toa

candy shop...where bon-bons... Homer CRASHES through the wall. tLulled by her sweet voice, he listens to the girl star for a few beats, then grabs her and EATS her, as she SCREAMS.

EXT. POLICE GARAGE

‘30‘s motorcycles and police cars POUR OUT and head right. The last one out turns left.

INT. POLICE CAR - CONTINUOUS POLICE CHIEF WIGGUM is at the wheel. LOU

Uh Chief, the monster is that way.

9F0O4 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

WIGGUM (AFRAID OF HOMER) I know what I’m doing. EXT. EMPIRE STATE BUILDING - NIGHT - ESTABLISHING INT. EMPIRE STATE BUILDING - CONTINUOUS Burns and Marge sit on a bed. BURNS I’m dreading the reviews, I can tell you that. Homer’s giant head appears at the window. MARGE Oh hi, Homie. His giant hand reaches in the window and grabs Marge. EXT. EMPIRE STATE BUILDING - A LITTLE LATER Homer is climbing the building with a SCREAMING Marge in his fist. A sweating Homer is HUFFING and PUFFING from the climb. He looks up and sees he has fifty stories left to climb. HOMER (MOANS) TOP OF BUILDING Three biplanes are circling. There is no sign of Homer. INT. BIPLANE PILOT He’s sure taking his sweet time. CO-PILOT Better refuel.

The planes HEAD OFF.

29.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BACK TO HOMER He looks winded. MARGE You know, you look a little flushed. Maybe you should eat more vegetables and less people. HOMER (GRUNTS "UH-HUH")

A GASPING Homer sets Marge down on a ledge, caresses her once, then lets go of the building.

EXT. EMPIRE STATE BUILDING - WIDE SHOT

We see Homer is only two stories up. He falls to the ground with a THUD.

EXT. STREET A CROWD has gathered to see Homer’s body. BURNS It wasn’t beauty that killed the beast. ‘Twas the fall... and that trolley car that smashed into the small of his back. HOMER (GROANS ) Marge runs up. MARGE

He’s not dead.

30.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BURNS No, but his career is. I remember when Al Jolson ran amok at the Winter Garden and climbed the Chrysler Building. After that, he couldn’t get arrested in this town.

HOMER (MOANS)

Burns and the reporters walk away. Marge gently strokes Homer’s huge head.

MARGE Don’t worry, Homie. I/’11 take care of you. We can make a new start. Homer COUGHS up the child star’s dress. DISSOLVE TO: INT. CHURCH - DAY

Homer, in a huge tux, and Marge, in a wedding gown, are getting married. A MONKEY enters and walks up to an USHER.

USHER Are you with the bride or groom? MONKEY (MONKEY NOISES) USHER Right this way. He leads the monkey over to the side of the church that is

filled with APES. The other side is filled with MARGE’S FAMILY.

31.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

MRS. BOUVIER (LOOKING VERY UNHAPPY) Marge does these things just to spite me. DISSOLVE TO: INT. CHURCH - FRONT - A LITTLE LATER Marge and Homer’s families are lined up for a wedding photo. (NOTE: THE WEDDING CAKE HAS A GIANT APE GROOM AND NORMAL BRIDE ON TOP.) Homer’s family is picking lice out of each other’s fur. PHOTOGRAPHER Folks, if you could just stop cleaning each other for a second... He starts to SNAP photographs. Homer GROWLS. MRS. BOUVIER Wait! Wait! Marge, I can’t find your father.

Homer has a leg sticking out his mouth. He SLURPS it in like spaghetti and BURPS sheepishly.

MARGE (EXASPERATED) Oh, Homer.

FADE OUT.

32.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

ACT THREE: "ZOMBIES" FADE IN: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM Marge enters with a tray. MARGE Now kids, instead of candy, I thought I’d serve an array of healthy fresh fruits. KIDS (OMINOUS MUTTERING) MARGE Fruit is nature’s candy! KIDS (LOUDER MUTTERING) An ashtray flies past Marge and HITS the wall. MARGE Whoa! Suddenly, the door BURSTS open. A MAN covered in blood, with his decapitated head hanging by a thread from his blood-spurting neck staggers in, MOANING. The kids SCREAM. Flanders’ head pops up from the neck hole. FLANDERS (LAUGHING) Hi fellow Halloweenies! Did I scaredly-dare ya? Grampa is clutching his chest. GRAMPA

(HEART ATTACK NOISES)

33.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BART Sorry, Mr. Flanders, but the scariest thing I’ve seen tonight is Emperor Cue-Ball over there. (POINTS TO

HOMER) HOMER

(CHUCKLES) Emperor Cue-Ball... Hey! BART

But I’ve got a story so scary you/ll wet your pants. GRAMPA Too late. Kids around Grampa scoot away. DISSOLVE TO: INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY = BART’S CLASSROOM = DAY

Bart stands up front, reading a crudely printed book report and holding a toddler’s pop-up book.

BART From A-Apple to Z-Zebra, "Baby’s First Pop-Up Book" is twenty-six pages of alphabetic adventure. H, J, R -- They’re all here, as well as some old friends you may have

forgotten -- like "F".

34.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

MRS. KRABAPPEL Bart, you mean to tell me that you read a book intended for preschoolers? BART Well, most of it. MRS. KRABAPPEL You have to read another book. ZOOM IN on Bart’s stricken face. INT. SCHOOL LIBRARY - A LITTLE LATER Bart picks up "Find Waldo Yet Again". Bart looks at a page showing three people on a beach. One of them is Waldo. He is waving. [DESIGN NOTE: This should not exactly look like Waldo. ] BART Man, he’s just not trying anymore. (SEES) Whoa! The "Occult" section is lit by a skull with a candle in it. Bart brushes aside some cobwebs and walks in. A faint HEARTBEAT can be heard, along with scattered DISTANT WAILS. A rat scurries back into his hole. Bart scans the titles; the Dewey decimal numbers are all 666.

A book behind him wriggles its way out of the stack and HITS Bart in the back of the head.

BART (CONT’D) Whoa! ow! What’s this? The cover reads, "The Time-Life Booke of Magick and Spelles." Bart opens it to an illustration of featureless WAILING faces. The faces project out from the page one by one, a la the children trapped inside Freddy Krueger. FACES Evil... Madness... Beware...

Beware...

35.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BART Cool. Bart SNAPS the book shut on the wailing faces. FACES (V.0.) Ow! Ow! Ow! ow! INT. SIMPSON HOUSE = LISA’S BEDROOM = EVENING

Lisa SIGHS as she pets Snowball II and sadly looks at a photo of Snowball I. Bart is reading from his spells book.

BART (READING) Chapter eight, "Let’s Talk Zombies..." If a zombie bites you, you become a zombie. You must walk the earth feeding on the brains of the living until the spell is broken.

Lisa points to the cat’s photo.

LISA Bart, please. Don’t you remember that Snowball I died four years ago tonight? Run over by the Mayor’s beer swilling brother, Clovis.

BART Hey, maybe there’s a spell in here that’1l bring her back from the dead.

Bart opens the book.

36.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

BART (CONT'D) Let’s see what we’ve got... "How to Get Your Skeletons Their Whitest"... "Selling Your Soul in a Buyer’s Market"... Ah, here we are, "How to Raise The Dead." (FIENDISH CHUCKLE) EXT. PET CEMETERY - EVENING PAN from the human cemetery next door to a sign reading "PET CEMETERY." One pet grave has a statue of hamster in a wire wheel. Another has a tombstone with a picture of a lobster which says, "EATEN BY MISTAKE". Other tombstones read, "CAPITOL CRITTERS," "FISH POLICE," and "FAMILY DOG." Over by Snowball I’s grave, Bart wears a sheet and the "Thriller" record album cover stuck on his head to resemble a wizard. The book is open in front of him. A strong wind begins to blow. BART (CHANTS) Prozac, Xanax, Darvon, Tums! In the human cemetery, lightning STRIKES the tombstones. They start trying to uproot themselves. The ground CRACKS. Light shines up from underground. BART (CONT’D) (CHANTS) Cullen, Rayburn, Narz, Trebek! In the human cemetery, a hand erupts from a grave. BART (CONT’D) (CHANTS) Zabar, Kresge, Caldor, Walmart! All over the human cemetery, CORPSES are climbing out of

graves. In the pet cemetery there is no motion. Lisa SIGHS, then looks over to the human cemetery.

37.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

LISA Bart! You cast the wrong spell! Zombies! BART Please Lis, they prefer to be called the Living Impaired. EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - NIGHT Willie finishes spreading mulch on a flower bed. WILLIE There. Pretty as a picture. A zombie erupts from the ground, messing up the mulch. WILLIE (CONT’D) Ach! Zombies. (SMOOTHS) There. Pretty as a picture. EXT. STREET - NIGHT Bart and Lisa, pursued by Zombies, ride their bikes into the garage and run into their house. Several Zombies KNOCK on the FLANDERS’ door. FLANDERS (OPENING DOOR) Howdy strangers! What can I do ya for? ZOMBIES Brains... Brains... The zombies push him in. As Flanders closes the door: FLANDERS (0.S.) Oh, why, Bill Dokes, you rascal! I thought you were dead -- (SCREAMS)

38.

9FO4 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92 39.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - EVENING

Bart and Lisa rush in. HOMER is sitting on the couch watching TV.

LISA (BREATHLESSLY) Dad, we did something very bad. HOMER Did you wreck the car? BART No. HOMER Did you raise the dead? LISA Yes. HOMER But the car is okay? Bart and Lisa nod. BART/LISA Uh huh. HOMER (CONT’D) All right then. Bart and Lisa give each other a look. INT. POLICE STATION - NIGHT CHIEF WIGGUM answers the phone. WIGGUM

Y’ello.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

INTERCUT - LISA ON PHONE

LISA Hello, police? A plague of zombies has risen from the grave!

WIGGUM (SNICKERS) Little girl, if there were zombies in this town, I think I’d know about it. I do have a brain in my head, you know.

This catches the interest of several ZOMBIE POLICE officers. They approach, GROWLING.

ON LISA WIGGUM (V.0.) Hey fellas, wha... what are you doing? Give that back! (PLEADING) Well, at least let me have a taste. A shocked Lisa hangs up. EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - DAY INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - BART’S CLASSROOM

The students are working when PRINCIPAL SKINNER‘S voice comes over the P.A.

SKINNER (V.O.) Martin Prince, report to my office at once! INTERCUT = SKINNER’S OFFICE

Skinner is now a Zombie.

40.

9FO4 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

SKINNER ZOMBIE And bring that big, juicy, Chess Club brain of yours along with you! (LICKS CHOPS) BACK TO CLASS ROOM MARTIN starts to leave. MARTIN (NERVOUS NOISE) ON TV KRUSTY stands in front of a gift-wrapped box. KRUSTY Hey kids, look what you can win in the Krusty Sweepstakes! He opens the box. ZOMBIE SIDESHOW MEL reaches out, GROWLING. Krusty SCREAMS as Mel pulls him in. We see a "TECHNICAL DIFFICULTIES -- PLEASE STAND BY" art card with a drawing of a drunken camera man holding a big liquor bottle behind the camera. When the picture returns, Zombie Krusty, looking disheveled, stares glassy-eyed at the camera. ZOMBIE KRUSTY To enter, send me your parents’ brains, or write "PARENTS’ BRAINS" on a 3-by-5 card, and send it to... The contest address comes on screen. PULL BACK to:

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

The Simpsons are worriedly watching TV. Zombies start SMASHING their arms through the window as in Night of the Livi Dead.

MARGE

Homer, did you barricade the door?

41.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

HOMER Why? Oh, the zombies. No.

Zombies SMASH through the door. Homer steps in front of his family.

HOMER (CONT’D) (HEROICALLY) Spare my family. Take me. Take me! The family runs upstairs. The zombies examine Homer. One peers through his ear. Another taps his head -- we hear a HOLLOW MELON SOUND. The zombies toss Homer aside. ZOMBIES Brains! Brains! They walk off. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - MASTER BEDROOM - NIGHT The family huddles nervously as Homer rushes in. BART Well, I learned one thing from all this: Don’t go to the library. Just don’t. LISA Wait Bart! Maybe the library has another book that’ll reverse the spell. MARGE It’s our only hope. Homer pulls out a shotgun from behind the headboard. He spins it around his finger and cocks it with one fluid motion.

HOMER

To the book depository!

42.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

EXT. SIMPSON HOUSE - NIGHT

Homer, Marge (carrying Maggie), Bart and Lisa hurry out. ZOMBIE FLANDERS walks over.

ZOMBIE FLANDERS Hey Simpson, I’m feeling a mite peckish. Mind if I chew your ear?

With the camera off Flanders, we see Homer raise his gun and FIRE. Bart and Lisa GASP.

BART Dad, you killed the zombie Flanders! HOMER He was a zombie? EXT. STREET - NIGHT

Homer drives through streets filled with wandering zombies. He hits several and they bounce off his hood.

SINGERS (V.0.) KZMB! All zombie radio. ZOMBIE D.J.’S (V.0.) (GROWLS) EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - NIGHT The Simpsons’ car pulls up. They sneak into the front door. ZOMBIES DOLPH, JIMBO and KEARNEY are playing a very slow- moving game of kickball with Principal Skinner’s head. SKINNER Ow... careful... not the face. INT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL = HALLWAY The camera is framed tightly on the Simpsons as they start

toward the staircase. Suddenly they run into ZOMBIE MRS. KRABAPPEL.

43.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

ZOMBIE MRS. KRABAPPEL Bart Simpson, I told you I wanted your brain on my desk last Friday! BART Uh... my dog ate it. ZOMBIE MRS. KRABAPPEL (INHUMANLY LOUD) Liar! She points her finger and ROARS like the body snatchers from the remake. Zombies appear from everywhere. The kids run up the staircase; Homer starts BLASTING with his shotgun. He spots Barney munching on an arm. HOMER Barney! Not you, too! BARNEY I’m not a zombie. But hey, when in Rome... (POINTS) Wow, George Washington.

ZOMBIE GEORGE WASHINGTON advances on Homer, arms raised. Homer SHOOTS him.

HOMER Take that Washington! ZOMBIE ALBERT EINSTEIN approaches. Homer SHOOTS him too. HOMER (CONT’D) Eat lead, Einstein! ZOMBIE WILLIAM SHAKESPEARE approaches and Homer tries to

shoot him. He is out of bullets so he BASHES Shakespeare in the face and stomach repeatedly with the butt of his

gun. HOMER (CONT’D)

Show’s over, Shakespeare!

44.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

ZOMBIE SHAKESPEARE The rest is silence. Zombie Shakespeare dies. INT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY - LIBRARY Bart is going through the occult stacks. Zombie arms BREAK holes in the door. Just as Bart finds the book, the zombies BREAK down the door. Bart grabs a book and opens it. BART (READING) Kolchak, Mannix, Banacek, Danno!

Lisa turns into a GIANT SNAIL. Her eyes are on stalks. She still wears pearls. Bart is shocked. He GASPS.

LISA What’s wrong? BART (COVERING) Uh, I never realized what a beautiful young woman you’ve become. (READING) Trojan, Ramses, Magnum, Sheik! Light EXPLODES from the book. Lisa changes back to herself. The zombies shield their eyes, then slowly turn and head out the door. In an OVERHEAD SHOT, we see the power of the spell swoop through town. GRAVEYARD Zombies are climbing back into their graves. A Pilgrim zombie stops a cowboy zombie who’s climbing into a grave marked "John Smith, 1833-1882". PILGRIM ZOMBIE Excuse me. I’m John Smith. COWBOY ZOMBIE

(RE: TOMBSTONE) John Smith, 1882?

45.

9F04 FINAL DELIVERY 5/01/92

PILGRIM ZOMBIE My mistake.

The zombies climb into the graves and pull dirt onto themselves.

EXT. CITY HALL - DAY Quimby addresses the CROWD. QUIMBY The zombies that plagued our town are now just corpses rotting in our streets. CROWD Yay! INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT The crisis is over. The Simpsons are watching TV. MARGE Well, I’m sure glad we didn’t turn into mindless zombies. BART Shh! (POINTS) TV!

From the TV we hear a SITCOM LAUGH TRACK. The Simpsons eat and watch mindlessly for a long beat.

HOMER Man... fall down. (BEAT) Funny. SIMPSONS (ZOMBIE-LIKE GRUNT OF APPROVAL) The Simpsons continue to watch and eat. FADE OUT.

THE END

46.
